narrative:
On 21 May 2026, the Karnataka High Court heard 257 of 423 scheduled cases across three courtrooms, yielding an overall hearing rate of **60.8%** — a marked improvement over 36.4% on 19 May and 44.3% on 30 April, though today's docket was considerably smaller (423 vs. 876 and 1,657 cases respectively).

Among the three benches, **SURAJ GOVINDARAJ & K. MANMADHA RAO** (Hall 9) led with a 72.3% hearing rate on 47 scheduled matters. **K. V. ARAVIND** (Hall 11) cleared 60.5% of 185 cases, while **C. M. POONACHA** (Hall 10) addressed 58.1% of the largest docket at 191 cases. Notably, Poonacha's rate stands well below his 79.2% on 19 May, when he carried a lighter load of 125 cases, suggesting a volume-driven decline rather than a structural shift.

Stage-wise, **ORDERS** converted at the highest rate — 76.3% (90 of 118) — consistent with its strong 70.8% on 19 May and reinforcing that cases at the orders stage are reliably disposed. **PRELIMINARY HEARING** (33.3%) and **ADMISSION** (44.3%) continued to lag, indicating these early-stage matters face persistent scheduling slippage.

By case type, **Writ Petitions (WP)** dominated the docket at 220 scheduled, clearing at 58.2% — similar to 61.5% on 19 May and slightly above 36.1% on 30 April. **Criminal Petitions (CRL.P)** performed better at 63.7%. **Writ Appeals (WA)** saw a dramatic recovery to 76.9% today, compared to just 5.0% on 19 May when 397 WA matters were listed but only 20 heard — today's cohort of 13 was far more manageable. Smaller dockets like CP, WPHC, COMAP, RP, and RSA achieved full clearance at 100%.
